candidates.stmt: As a former legal aid lawyer, a lecturer at London South Bank University and a community organiser in Haringey, I recognise the difficulties of juggling family, work and political hope for something better. I fight for everyone who struggles; I want the old, the young, those who are busy and those who are lonely to thrive. Everyone counts. Disrepair, ASB, and protecting and enhancing our green spaces are our main focusses; I’ll bring years of experience, including two decades in crime and family law, to solving our communities’ issues.

As a long-time resident of Alexandra Park, this community means the world to me, and my family.

My genuine connection to our local area means a first hand experience/understanding of local issues, and a vested interest to ensure resident-informed solutions and bold actions on local issues such as; traffic, fly tipping in certain corners, theft and anti-social behaviour, housing disrepair which affects our neighbours in council properties, ensuring that no tenant lives in damp and mould infested property, making our pavements safer especially for our elderly, installing lightening in our parks to make them safer for all residents especially women and young people during the dark winter months, continue our investment in local playgrounds for young families, securing step free access for Alexandra Palace Station for all but especially parents like me with pushchairs and our disabled neighbours, continue the protection of vital community assets such as our Alexandra Park library, ensuring that the library remains open and expanding activities and restoring full opening hours, protecting of our green spaces including green corridors like Pinkham Way.
It is also vital to maintain and improve the quality of services that are already being delivered well, such as our bin collections.

Employment and skills support for our young people, especially those not in education or employment (NEETS), as well as struggling families still facing difficulties due to the devastating impacts of Covid Pandemic and the austerity imposed by the Conservatives/Liberal Democrats coalition government.

At work in a London Local Authority, I stand up to developers and their contractors. As London changes and high rises shoot up, it’s vital that developers give back to the communities they profit from. This could be in the form of employment and skills and support for local services et cetera.

I’d be honoured to represent you, to ensure that those of us who live in this wonderful community are

An experienced local campaigner who gets things done and will be a strong voice for our area

I'm a proud resident of Haringey having lived and worked here for the past twenty years. During that time I have a strong record of campaigning on local issues and standing up for residents both inside and outside of politics.

As a former Haringey councillor serving as Opposition Lead for Finance for four years, and having worked for former MP Lynne Featherstone, I not only understand how the council works, but I have great experience in providing the help and support many people in Haringey need, and in getting things done for the community.

I have been actively involved in local life through Alexandra Park Cricket Club, both as a player and as Treasurer. Alongside my professional career leading a successful UK business, I'm committed to giving back to the community and working hard for local residents.

Priorities for Alexandra Park

1) Your Community
To continue Cllr Alessandra's tireless work campaigning to keep our wonderful local library open, and look to reverse the reduction in opening hours imposed by Labour. Work closely with Alexandra Palace to ensure community needs are represented around major events, and liaise regularly with the police to prioritise community safety through improved street and park lighting, patrols, and engagement. And continue to keep residents like you updated all year round.

2) Your Streets
Continue the work of our local Lib Dem councillors in delivering safer streets, with more pedestrian crossings to add to those our Lib Dem councillors here before me achieved. Take a locally-informed and evidence-based approach to tackle the congestion and traffic volumes in our area.

Tackle speeding to make our roads safer, lead on improving transport for all, without compromising parking and the access needs of residents, and, following our success in securing the upcoming works to add step-free access at Alexandra Palace Station, fight for acces

Michael Anderson lives in the Warehouse District in Hermitage & Gardens and has become deeply involved in community campaigns in his 6+ years living in Haringey.

He helped set up the successful Save The Warehouses campaign, stopping the demolition of Omega Works (where over 100 people live), and is part of the Warehouse Residents Network. He works in politics as a communications and campaigns manager.

As councillor, he will work alongside residents to protect the beauty of Alexandra Park, ensuring that the ward and park are safe for all. He will listen to residents on how to make transport and traffic control work better here, and protect and empower renters.

Brij has been involved in the Haringey community for over 20 years, including serving as a parent governor and deputy chair of governors at a local school. That experience has given him direct insight into the pressures facing local families, schools and public services. Professionally, he has worked at the BBC, the Department for Culture Media and Sport, Ofcom, in academia, and now at Deloitte, where he challenges tech companies to make safer products.

As a Green councillor, Brijesh will leverage his experience to strengthen services, support schools and make sure council decisions are informed and rooted in residents’ needs.

We are not career politicians and **we are not bound by party positions or internal discipline**. We live in the area we seek to represent, and we care deeply about its future. We became involved through everyday local issues - changes to streets, transport and parking - where many residents felt decisions were being made without our voices being heard or properly considered.

Like others locally, we engaged with meetings and consultations, but too often it felt as though outcomes were decided in advance. Over time, it became clear that borough-wide priorities and party lines were frequently taking precedence over what works best for Alexandra Park ward.

We believe **our neighbourhood deserves councillors whose first responsibility is to their community**, not to party structures. That is why we are standing as Independents - free to question proposals, challenge poor decisions, and **put Alexandra Park residents first**.

We are standing as independent candidates focused on everyday issues in our area:

- Protect local parking against further expansion of CPZs
- Support balanced transport policies that work for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists alike
- Safer streets, repaired roads and pavements, and better lighting
- Push for step-free access at Alexandra Palace Station
- Protect Alexandra Park Library from cuts
- Protect Pinkham Way green space
- No to building over Muswell Hill car parks
- Ensure transparency and accountability in the Council’s decision-making processes

**Realistic promise**: We can’t fix it all but we will speak up for residents, ask difficult questions, challenge decisions that do not stand up to scrutiny, and make the voices of our neighbourhood heard where it matters.

- No wider political agenda
- No party whip
- Just local people standing up for the community

We are not career politicians and **we are not bound by party positions or internal discipline**. We live in the area we seek to represent, and we care deeply about its future. We became involved through everyday local issues - changes to streets, transport and parking - where many residents felt decisions were being made without our voices being heard or properly considered.

Like others locally, we engaged with meetings and consultations, but too often it felt as though outcomes were decided in advance. Over time, it became clear that borough-wide priorities and party lines were frequently taking precedence over what works best for Alexandra Park ward.

We believe **our neighbourhood deserves councillors whose first responsibility is to their community**, not to party structures. That is why we are standing as Independents - free to question proposals, challenge poor decisions, and **put Alexandra Park residents first**.

We are standing as independent candidates focused on everyday issues in our area:

- No wider political agenda
- No party whip
- Just local people standing up for the community

It’s been an honour to represent Bounds Green since 2022. I know our area’s challenges and strengths. I’m standing again to continue championing our residents, schools, and local businesses. Congestion and road safety at Bounds Green junction remain urgent. I’ve successfully campaigned for safer crossings and better traffic management, and I’m committed to improving parks, supporting local businesses, and tackling antisocial behaviour, litter, and fly-tipping. Bounds Green deserves visible councillors who get results. With Andrea, I’ll continue delivering a fairer, greener, and safer community.

My priority is creating opportunity and fairness for every resident. I’ve lived next door in Alexandra Park for 30 years and love our community. Locally, I’ve been on a neighbourhood ward panel and worked as a school governor and teacher. I will take the experience these roles gave me in representing my community to tackle local issues alongside Cllr Emily Arkell. As a councillor, I’ll maintain improvements at Bounds Green station’s junction and support Myddleton Road market. We’ll take antisocial behaviour and fly-tipping seriously and together we’ll create a greener Finsbury Gardens.

Dan is a Bounds Green local, bringing up his family here, and campaigning hard for the area he calls home.

He founded and led a society that brought 550 residents together to **create the Hillbilly Social on Myddleton Road**, and has helped local residents with serious **housing issues**.

This has included taking the fight to Labour-run Haringey Council over their use of unscrupulous HMO landlords in Bounds Green, a story which attracted national coverage in The Guardian.

**Dan has a track record of bringing positive change to Bounds Green**. He is tired of residents being ignored by the council and wants to ensure your voice is heard.

**Our Priorities for Bounds Green**

**Competence**
We both live right here in Bounds Green, and are running to be your local councillors because we care about fixing the basics.

From dealing with dangerous junctions to fixing lighting and pavements, we will stand up for Bounds Green.

We need a council, and local councillors, that help, listen, and respond to residents' concerns.

**Compassion**
We will keep residents safe by cracking down on rogue landlords, fixing dangerous defects and ending damp and mould in social housing and pushing to restore the size of the police team in Bounds Green.

**Community**
After leading the creation of our community pub, we will take that energy to reinvigorate our parks and public spaces, listening to residents, and putting their voices at the heart of our actions.

Asli has lived in Bounds Green for the past 18 years, and cares deeply about the local area and the wonderful community we have here.

As a seasoned, professional interpreter, Asli has developed a profound understanding of the local issues and the specific needs of her neighbours. Her extensive experience enables her to connect with community members on a personal level, fostering trust and open communication.

In addition to her interpreting work, she is deeply committed to advocating for families facing housing challenges, recognising the vital link between stable housing, health, and education.

Asli is passionate about empowering those around her, ensuring that everyone has access to the resources and support they need to thrive.

**Our Priorities for Bounds Green**

**Competence**
We both live right here in Bounds Green, and are running to be your local councillors because we care about fixing the basics.

From dealing with dangerous junctions to fixing lighting and pavements, we will stand up for Bounds Green.

We need a council, and local councillors, that help, listen, and respond to residents' concerns.

**Compassion**
We will keep residents safe by cracking down on rogue landlords, fixing dangerous defects and ending damp and mould in social housing and pushing to restore the size of the police team in Bounds Green.

**Community**
After leading the creation of our community pub, we will take that energy to reinvigorate our parks and public spaces, listening to residents, and putting their voices at the heart of our actions.

It’s been an honour to be your Councillor for the last four years. I’m a local dad, and I volunteer with many fantastic Bruce Castle groups. I’m re-standing as I love our corner of Tottenham, our green spaces, unique heritage buildings and community spirit, and want to continue to be your voice on the council. I’ve led on many local campaigns, from identifying and securing sites for new council homes, to the largest street tree planting in a generation. Together with Haringey Labour, we’ve introduced new toilets at White Hart Lane Station, protected our local library opening hours, and secured record funding for Bruce Castle Park, Playground and Museum.

I’ve raised my family in Tottenham for over 25 years whilst working in the borough, teaching and supporting SEND young people and children. As Councillor, I’ve worked tirelessly on housing and with Haringey’s rivers forum. As Mayor of Haringey from May 2024-2025, I had the pleasure of being invited to hundreds of community group events. I’m proud that many were within the ward, namely Bruce Castle and The Trampery. Our Labour council has excellent, courageous leadership - pioneering initiatives to tackle the climate crisis and structural inequality. I’d love to continue working with them to make Haringey an even better place.

I feel inseparable to our local community, which has been home to me and my family for over 10 years. Over the years, I have driven local Labour Party initiatives to improve social housing, enhance green spaces, and make our streets safer for everyone. I am a proud Unison member and will serve our community with care and enthusiasm. I’m also passionate about renewable energy and dedicated to promoting a sustainable environment. I would treasure the opportunity to work with you all to make Bruce Castle an even more wonderful place to live, work, and visit.

Engaging in community work with young people in Tottenham, it's clear that we need youth centres, lower rents and actual social services. For these reasons and for you, I am standing as a Haringey Socialist Alliance candidate on 7 May 2026.
 
We are an alliance of socialists from local grassroots community groups, campaigning groups in the Borough and local community activists. We are committed to transforming local politics in Haringey. We aspire to build an inclusive and democratic alliance to provide a political alternative to the establishment in Haringey and to challenge the rotten Labour Party leadership of the Council that is imposing Austerity on residents. We want to join with and amplify the voices of social justice campaigns across Haringey. We oppose all cuts in services, the wealthy should pay for decent Public Services including housing, health, education. We oppose the privatisation of public services for profit!

On Housing we will:
Expand social home building and provide appropriate accommodation  for all our communities
Call off evictions and the bailiffs and enforce legislation against unlawful and unfair evictions
Campaign for rent controls
Make private landlords implement repairs and refurbishment to provide clean quality rented accommodation for all our residents
Make  a permanent plan to repair and improve all social housing
Change Haringey’s planning priorities to serve Haringey residents not  property developers and speculators
Stop selling off Haringey Council property and assets

On Poverty we will:
Change the narrative! Those in poverty will not be blamed, we will blame those who are making themselves rich off the backs off the poor and the politicians who support the rich
Fight for justice for all benefits claimants and oppose all benefit cuts
Build local community hubs to build solidarity, agency and advocacy for our residents
Campaign for free public transport in London

On the Climate Catastrophe we will
Take all the necessary measure

People power will make the difference.I have worked for and delivered rent reductions in council housing. I will continue to advocate for local people and hold this Council to account.

Implement p

1. Fix disrepair in our community 
2. Ensure our pavements are accessible 
3. Remove abandoned and illegally parked E-Bikes
4. Fix pot holes properly 
5. Hold to account those who have failed
6. I will be your voice and will speak up , so you do not suffer in silence

Dear Neighbour,

I am honoured to be seeking re-election in Bounds Green--having served the people of Fortis Green over these last 4 years. If elected, I will work tirelessly all year round for local N11 and N22 residents. I believe our local Liberal Democrat manifesto is both ambitious and positive. For example, we really want to put the 'Green' back into Bounds Green and promise a Green Revolution.  

I am a member of the local Friends of the Earth Group, and was an outspoken critic of the Edmonton Incinerator. I also want to see better cycling provisions in our Borough.

Haringey has been run by the Labour Party for over 50 years -- and they have a massive majority on the Council. What difference will yet more Labour councillors make?

Please vote for me and Ibrahim Hassan, my running mate, to reduce the democratic deficit in Haringey and to 'send a message' to Boris Johnson's government.

Thank you for reading. Please don't hesitate to contact me if you have a questions or grumbles.

Best wishes,

Cllr Justin Hinchcliffe (Lib Dem) justindavidhinchcliffe@gmail.com  07918 973 976

Dr Imad Ahmed
Imad has been a dedicated community campaigner for over 20 years. With a professional background in negotiating complex government investments and projects, he has the expertise to ensure Haringey Council truly delivers for residents.

Driven by a commitment to improving people’s lives, his experience ranges from international human rights through to local improvements, such as the greening of his own street with 14 new trees.

Imad will ensure our leisure centres and town centre are prioritised, protect our parks and libraries, and champion vocational training for all.

All posts promoted by K Riddle on behalf Imad Ahmed (Haringey Liberal Democrats) at Ashley House, 235-239 High Road, N22 8HF.

Tay Quartermain is a lifelong resident of Haringey and brings over 20 years’ experience in public service as a local NHS Nurse, including working for the community on the front line during the COVID-19 pandemic. He has experienced first-hand how underfunded services drive health inequalities across our borough. Driven by his decades-long commitment to the people of Haringey, Tay is running to create a greener, fairer borough and to take a stand against the politics of division. If elected, Tay will fight tirelessly for the people of Haringey.

Rimil Hembrom has lived in Haringey for more than four years and has been inspired by the borough’s strong tradition of solidarity and resistance to division. A first-generation migrant, active trade unionist and long-time campaigner, he wants to build on that culture of care in local government. Rimil is standing because he believes the council should be more open, more democratic and more closely connected to the communities it serves. He wants to create more ways for residents to shape local decisions and will support stronger grassroots networks across the borough. His priorities include participation, mutual support, dignity, and protecting the local environment for future generations.

Politics for Rimil are about looking out for the most vulnerable people and ensuring that the local infrastructure and services work in their favour, providing support they need. If elected, he will be a hard-working local councillor who is there for local residents to support them with their issues.

**Dushka Wertenbaker-Man** _(they/them)_ has lived around Crouch End all their life and is deeply rooted in the community. As an Assistant Pub Manager, they hear daily from residents about the pressures people face. A veteran campaigner, Dushka first got involved fighting tuition fee hikes and has since raised funds for Medical Aid for Palestinians and Refugee Action. For the Greens, they are active across local campaigns and community organising. Bilingual in French and speaking conversational Spanish, they understand how inaccessible systems can feel for many. They are committed to representing everyone in Crouch End while being a visible voice for LGBTQ+ residents. Their priorities include strong local services and councillors who show up, listen and act.

Dawn Barnes was elected to Haringey Council in Crouch End ward in May 2018. She contested Enfield and Haringey at the GLA elections in 2012, and stood against David Cameron in the 2010 General Election.

Dawn lives in Bounds Green, Haringey, and has worked in communications and marketing for over ten years. Her experience is diverse and she has worked in the private, public and charity sectors including for a charity working with people who misuse substances and alcohol; a local council and a sustainable design and regeneration consultancy. She currently works for King's College London working to recruit students from a broad range of backgrounds.

Dawn hails from the north of England and came to London by way of France, Japan and Oxford. She was determined to live in the city from a very young age and fell in love with London at first sight. She has been a trustee of a national charity and co-manages a network of reading groups across London.

Having lived in Fortis Green for many years, I’m standing as a Labour candidate as I care about our neighbourhood and want to make it an even better place to live. Politics should focus on listening to residents to understand local issues to get them sorted. I’ll focus on everyday issues like street safety, schools, transport, housing, and keeping public spaces well looked after. I’d be a councillor who is approachable, reliable, and visible. I will be a strong voice for Fortis Green on Haringey Council, standing with Labour and working for the people who live here.

I’m proud to stand as a Labour candidate in Fortis Green, having lived locally for 25 years. As a project manager professionally and a community activist, our standard of living and how we care for and educate our children are my priorities. I will work hard to ensure residents are really listened to. Haringey’s Labour Council is already making real change, supporting residents and families across the borough. All of our Children’s Services rated ‘Outstanding’ and 98% of schools are ‘Good’ or ‘Outstanding. But there’s always more to do.

Fortis Green deserves a visible voice that fights for you. Together, we’ll build a greener and safer Fortis Green.

I’ve lived in Muswell Hill and Fortis Green for 28 years and I’m proud to have participated in and contributed to the civic life of our community. I’ve worked in London as an architect for 30 years, and I was a Fortismere School Governor for 8 years. I’m committed to community programmes, including the MH Synagogue welfare programmes and the MH Foodbank. I fully support Haringey Labour Council’s aims to be fairer, greener and welcoming to all. I know I can step up, serve and be a strong voice for Fortis Green, helping residents to improve their lives and well-being.

Greg lives and works in Haringey. Formerly a PE teacher at a secondary school in Tottenham as well as Blanche Nevile which is on the Fortismere Campus, he both believes strongly in the potential of young people in the borough and is clear-eyed about the barriers many of them face. He now runs an education-focused start-up and remains closely connected to local schools, families and businesses. As a councillor, Greg would seek to use his listening skills and problem-solving mindset to tackle local issues – and particularly to improve opportunities for young people and support healthier communities.

Christyn Parkes lives in Haringey – having made her home in the UK 18 years ago after moving here from Canada. She is a Senior Lecturer in History Education at King’s College London, a director of the Feminist Gender Equality Network, and co-lead of the Teaching American Studies Network. Since joining the Greens, Christyn has worked on party campaigns in London and beyond, while becoming more involved in local issues and LGBTQ+ rights. She is standing to tackle high rents, improve safety on local streets, and support a borough that is welcoming and inclusive. In Christyn, Haringey residents will have a councillor who is thoughtful, hardworking, and firmly on their side.

Jo Kuper joined the Greens, inspired by a movement cutting through the politics of hate and division. A North Londoner and grandchild of refugees, she brings deep local knowledge and a lifelong commitment to social, environmental and racial justice. She sees the everyday struggles of Haringey residents, and will fight for better housing, greener spaces and a council that works for them.

From Greenpeace ships to work with Doctors Without Borders, and years of grassroots activism, she has stood with communities facing conflict and climate breakdown, defending Indigenous peoples’ and migrant rights.

Jo is here to listen to the community, as we build a fairer, greener, kinder Haringey for all.

Rosie Latchford has lived in Haringey for the past four years; it’s home and a community she loves deeply. Her family is from East London, where they worked and lived for generations as dockworkers. She’s seen how this city fails the diverse working-class communities that shape it.

Rosie has over ten years of experience organising for climate and social justice, including fighting to stop the expansion of London City Airport, and for a free Palestine.

Rosie sees May’s elections as a chance to put hope back into politics. She’s struggled herself with renting in Haringey, and the rising cost of living. She promises to fight for change local residents can really feel, whether that’s better housing and fairer rents, standing firm against austerity, investing in social care or tackling the climate crisis.

Since moving to Haringey in 2023, Marc Jenner has had to move home five times. Facing rent hikes, damp and mould, eviction threats, and insecure employment, Marc understands the challenges facing working people in the borough.

Marc is seeking election in Harringay, the ward he has come to call home. Marc is an experienced campaigner and organiser, having worked with Palestine Solidarity Campaign and Acorn, here in Haringey. He will fight for better housing and stronger renter protections, clean streets, better investment in social care, green and accessible travel, and seek full council divestment from Israeli arms manufacture.

Marc loves Haringey. Its history reflects his values: culture, inclusivity, and working-class organising. Above all, Marc will prioritise fostering the community here, helping to deliver a Haringey they can be proud to call home.

Four years ago, you trusted me to serve Harringay Ward. I have never taken that responsibility lightly. I fought plans to scrap daily parking permits, opposed 24/7 gambling shops on Green Lanes, secured new road safety measures on Allison and Endymion Roads, and worked with the police to tackle intimidating behaviour on Falkland Road. I believe every child deserves the best start in life. I’ve worked with the Mayor of London to deliver local free school meals, and serve as the Council’s governor in a federation of Haringey nursery schools.

It’s been an honour representing Harringay’s diverse and strong community for nine years. As cabinet member, I’ve secured investment in our schools and improvements across children’s services. Listening to residents is vital, but so is action. I’ve challenged controversial planning and licensing, and I work with police and council officers to tackle drug dealing, fly‑tipping and other ASB which affect residents’ quality of life every day. I’m also proud we’ve secured funding to repave roads on the Ladder and install bike hangars. I’d love to keep fighting for Harringay as your local Labour Councillor.

I have a proven track record of achieving positive change through politics. I’ve volunteered with Amnesty International, a Kurdish human rights campaign, and with Holocaust survivors’ descendants. At Haringey Council, I worked on our successful bid for Borough of Culture 2027, secured safety improvements to Finsbury Park, and shaped policy that empowers community organisations to provide high quality activities and support for all of our communities. I’m proud to stand with Haringey Labour’s committed councillors who fight for our communities.

Hello, I have lived on the ladder all of my life and have experienced lots of the cuts to our public services first hand under both Tory and Labour austerity. We can beat this by coming together as a community and fighting for the funding and services we need which is something only TUSC stands for, we wont reverse austerity by carrying out more austerity as is either explicitly or implicity promised by every other party.

Every TUSC candidate is mandated to run on these minimum policy platforms:
1. Oppose all cuts and closures to council services, jobs, pay and conditions. Resist the privatisation of services or their transfer to social enterprises or ‘arms-length’ management organisations which are the first steps to their privatisation.

2. Reject council tax, rent and service charge increases for working-class people to make up for cuts in central funding.

3. Vote for councils to use their reserves and prudential borrowing powers to avoid making cuts in their 2026-2027 budgets and demand from the Labour government the additional funding needed to make up any future shortfall.

4. Refuse to co-operate with any commissioners or ‘envoys’ appointed by the government to attempt to impose cuts on local services.

5. Support only democratically debated local Climate Emergency plans that create new employment, build flood defences, reduce emissions and improve air quality and the local environment, while protecting the jobs, pay and living standards of all workers.

6. Fight for united working-class struggle against racism, sexism and all forms of oppression, including against refugees, asylum seekers and migrants.  Back all workers’ struggles against government policies making ordinary people pay for the crisis.

I will of course work with local residents and allies to build this out.

I’m standing because Hermitage and Gardens deserves strong, local representation and a fairer, greener and more secure future. I work in energy policy, fighting to lower bills and help families access clean, affordable heating. The cost of living crisis and the climate crisis must be tackled together, with practical solutions that make life better now. I want more warm homes, safer and cleaner streets, thriving small businesses and properly funded public services in Hermitage and Gardens. I’ll stand up for renters and push for more genuinely affordable housing. Labour values guide me: fairness, solidarity and opportunity.

Living close by on the Harringay Ladder, I know the key local issues - from housing problems, crime and flytipping, to green spaces and transport. Our Haringey Labour Council is tackling these issues and more, putting democratic socialism into action - building over a 1000 council homes, investing in our parks, transforming Haringey Children’s Services, and cutting crime with new neighbourhood patrols. I work in clean energy research and I’m a governor at a local secondary school. I know how to prioritise when resources are tight - but I will always be ambitious for our part of Haringey borough. I’d be honoured to have your vote.

The local elections are a chance to protest at Sir Keir Starmer’s
Labour government. He promised change after 14 years of Tory policies
but our public services and general living standards haven’t seen
it.  Only the super-rich elite are doing well.  But opposing Starmer
is easy.  Almost every other candidate on May 7th will say they do
too. So the elections should also be about what kind of change we
want to see.  DON’T JUST PROTEST ON MAY 7TH...  IT’S OUR CHANCE TO
VOTE IT’S OUR CHANCE TO VOTE FOR SOCIALIST CHANGE! FOR SOCIALIST
CHANGE!  IT’S OUR CHANCE TO VOTE FOR SOCIALIST CHANGE!

Starting with the local public services we are electing councillors
to provide.  Rent controls and council house building; more youth
clubs, libraries and elderly care centres; free school meals for
all, school uniform grants and nurseries; grants for 16-18 year
olds in education; affordable leisure, arts and sports facilities.
These are just some of the policies councils could implement.
Properly funded, council services can change lives.  Candidates
from the establishment parties don’t give us that choice though,
as they put the interests of the wealthy 1% elite first.

The Tories starved councils of the funding they need and Sir Keir
Starmer’s Labour government is doing the same, with the plan to
curb special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) rights just
the latest example. Meanwhile Reform, also led by millionaires, are
Tories on steroids, only concerned to divide working class people
so that it’s harder for us to fight back.  But candidates from the
Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition (TUSC) are different – a
chance not just to protest on May 7th but to vote for socialist
change.

--- 
Amnon is a member of the Socialist Party.
He lived in Israel until 2005, where he founded Socialist Struggle Movement, a socialist organisation which which campaigns against Capitalism, the exploitation of the working class, and the oppression of the Palestinians.

in 2005 he moved to Lon

Eva Bell lives in Highgate and works in policy and research that focuses on improving public services. Her work has informed national health policy, and she regularly works with public servants and politicians to deliver better outcomes for people who rely on services every day.

Eva wants to bring her experience to bear at the local level, where council decisions have a direct effect on people’s lives. She is passionate about public and community ownership and as a Green Councillor would work to develop public-common partnerships – allowing our community spaces to be owned by the local people, and so improving their sustainability and responsiveness to residents’ needs.

I live in Highgate with my wife, calling the area home from 2020-22 and from 2023 onwards. Housing issues are my top priority, regardless of whether you own or rent. An active member of my block’s management committee, I have challenged high costs for residents and will continue to do so for voters across Haringey if elected. Diagnosed with autism at the age of 20, I understand how important accessible, dependable local services are. As a Green councillor, I will remain focused on affordable housing and will push the council to reduce inequalities in the borough.

It is a privilege to stand in Highgate.

I lived on Milton Road for 3 years, and I have made the local area my home for over 10 years.
 
My wife and I could not wish for a more supportive, beautiful area to bring up our young family; from enjoying Highgate Wood, the creativity and culture of Jacksons Lane, the historic High Street, to restorative walks along Parkland Walk.

It is vital Highgate has strong Labour representation; from supporting our most vulnerable residents, preserving its unique green character and building a thriving, vibrant ward with strong local public services to support and enhance lives.
 
If elected, I will bring a deep commitment to community, a clear understanding of Highgate’s priorities, and a determination to work constructively for all residents.

As a Haringey resident, I’m proud to live in a borough with a Labour council that stands up for renters like me, tackling dodgy landlords and building thousands of council homes. I’m a proud socialist and trade unionist, values shaped by growing up under a Labour government. I want every child in Highgate to benefit from the opportunities I did twenty years ago. In Highgate we’re lucky to have fantastic green spaces, great public transport links. David, Sofia and I will be part of a Labour council that invests in our parks, tackles ASB and works with the Labour government to fix our NHS.

1. Get the basics right
 - security
 - accountability
 - fairness
 - kindness
 - reward hard work
 - represent Haringey and the United Kingdom well
 - Do what the majority wants under the protection of the minority
 - decency
 - Audiatur et altera pars
 - fortiter in re - suaviter in modo - constanter in se

My name is Shahab Mossavat - one of your three Reform UK candidates in Highgate.
I have lived here for 17 years. My son was born at the Whittington. This is my community as much as it is yours.

And like you, I am angry.

Angry that our taxes are squandered on box-ticking and bureaucracy rather than the things that genuinely matter - **good schools, functioning libraries, safe streets, decent homes, and roads that aren't just a cash machine for the council.**

Angry that a Labour council and a Labour government - economically illiterate and financially reckless - continue to make life harder for ordinary people. For tenants like me. For families like yours.

I came to this country as an immigrant and found in Britain everything I believed in: **family, community, country**. I do not want to see those values hollowed out by an administration more interested in ideology than in you.

Reform UK will cut waste, scrap pointless diversity roles, and put your money back where it belongs - into the services that build real communities. No more working from home on your time. No more net-zero vanity projects while your bills go through the roof. No more ghost towns and dying high streets while councillors congratulate themselves.

Haringey needs change. **Haringey needs Reform**.

Please give us your support on 7th May.

_Shahab Mossavat - Reform UK, Highgate_

I have lived in Hornsey for over a decade, after moving to London to study in Haringey when I was 18. Since you first elected me as your Councillor in 2022, it has been a complete privilege to give back to my neighbours and residents who make Hornsey so special.

I’ve stood alongside residents in difficult moments, taken firm action on anti-social behaviour on Hornsey High Street, and fought for safer and cleaner streets.

We’ve secured huge investment for Hornsey schools and Priory Park, built new council homes, introduced School Streets, 20mph zones, upgraded lighting and roads, and backed local jobs.

I’m determined to keep up that progress, to make public services a helping hand, to fight for what residents deserve and to write Hornsey’s next chapter together.

I have lived in Hornsey for over 20 years and my boys attended Campsbourne School.

I am in the Labour Party because I believe that we do better when we act together, when we protect the things we share as a community. This Tory government and the Tory Lib Dem coalition before it, is decimating our valued services and taking us back to an era of exceptional inequality and abject poverty. I have a background in housing and public law and I bring a wealth of experience to help residents with their problems. I hope to be not just a member of the Hornsey community, but also your councillor, serving the residents of Hornsey and acting on your behalf and in your interests.

It has been a privilege to represent my community since my election in 2014

I have lived in the ward for the past 12 years and am happy to call Hornsey home.Since my election I have held regular surgeries, met and worked with community groups, and carried out casework on behalf of residents ranging from recycling to repairs, and from school admissions to housing issues. I have taken a special interest in supporting our looked after children and in education in the borough and am proud that 99% of our schools are now rated good or outstanding by Ofsted.While the Government continues to impose devastating cuts on local authorities, I believe that Labour Councils can still support some of the most vulnerable members of our community and reduce inequalities.

I am a historian, a former British Museum curator, and a trustee of a royally chartered learned society. I have lived in Haringey for 22 years and in Hornsey for 18, now raising my family here.

Over that time I have too often seen complacency, poor decision-making, and a lack of transparency from this Labour-run council. Hornsey deserves better. I want local people listened to, our community’s needs prioritised, and the area’s unique character protected. Since we last had Lib Dem councillors representing the ward, Hornsey has simply been one of a number of areas in Haringey taken for granted by Labour. It's time for change.

Let's have a fresh start for Haringey with the Lib Dems. Open, honest, and accountable; with our communities at the heart of the decisions we make. That's our commitment to you.

I am an architect, specialising in conservation, who has lived and worked here locally for 20 years.
I want to improve housing quality, public spaces and climate resilience by bringing long-term , resident focused thinking to local decisions - making Muswell Hill an even more special place.
As a Liberal Democrat, giving back to the community, amplifying residents’ voices and supporting those most in need are, of course, central to why I want to be your local champion: to follow in the long tradition of Lib Dem councillors in Muswell Hill, started nearly three decades ago by Lynne Featherstone and continued most recently with Councillor Pippa Connor.

I’ve lived in Muswell Hill for 30 years and care deeply about our community.

I’ve seen residents’ voices ignored, consultations fail to lead to action, and decisions imposed without explanation. I want to change that.

With senior finance, property, and governance experience as a partner in one of the UK's top four accountancy firms, and local campaign experience as part of Councillor Pippa Connor's team here in Muswell Hill, I'll work alongside residents to improve transparency and ensure Haringey Council listens, responds, and delivers better.

I love Muswell Hill. My family enjoys its atmosphere, shops, green spaces and school that my daughter attended. I was a local piano teacher for Haringey Music Service. I’m a member of the Labour Party because it represents my beliefs in social justice and equality. As Councillor, I attend as many community events and activities as I can. I strive to make living in the local community a better experience. I’ve successfully fought to restart the local youth club, secured longer opening hours for Muswell Hill Library, helped save the Rookfield Estate tennis courts from an overseas developer and got the hostels on Queens’ Avenue on the Council’s Major Works refurbishment programme.

Politics to me is taking actions to improve lives. A process that starts here in Muswell Hill, I want to provide opportunities for the younger generation, struggling families and older residents who live on their own. Ensuring every voice is heard and every problem addressed is my top priority. I’m proud to act locally, having worked closely with councillor Cathy Brennan and serving as elected councillor myself. My experience in the advice sector ensuring residents can access help when they’re most vulnerable has prepared me well. Together, we’re a strong team, willing and able to help our residents.

Florence Wright is a lifelong resident of Muswell Hill. Alongside completing her Master’s degree at the LSE in Societal and Environmental psychology, she works in Green MP Ellie Chowns’s Parliamentary office. Florence has been involved in climate justice campaigning for years, and locally coordinates the Eco Group at St James Church. She is also a long-term volunteer at Wave Hub Muswell Hill, an inclusive cafe for people with and without learning disabilities to socialise and work together. Her priorities include clean air, protecting green spaces, supporting local businesses, strengthening youth services, and making Muswell Hill more inclusive and accessible for everyone.

Josh Thomson has family roots in Muswell Hill going back several generations and is now raising his own family in Haringey.  Professionally, Josh works in the City as a management consultant specialising in environmental change – challenging banks to turn their commitments into reality. He will bring that same focus to local government in Muswell Hill. His priorities include protecting our green spaces, cleaning up the air our children breathe, and making sure residents have councillors who are accountable, ambitious and focused on building a fairer future for us all.

Simon Clark has lived in Noel Park with his husband and their two dogs for the past four years. He works as a Chartered Project Manager and director for a built environment engineering consultancy in the rail industry, handling major infrastructure projects. As such, he can operate effectively in complex environments and deal with public bodies. He has built deep roots in the community, serving as a school governor and as a trustee for a local charity which improves access to rowing for underprivileged and minoritised communities. He will put his experience to work for Noel Park residents, focusing on services, schools, the built environment and green spaces, and ensuring that public investment genuinely benefits the community.

I was elected as a Councillor in Noel Park in 2014 and am delighted to be a Labour candidate for the elections in May 2018.

As a lifelong resident of Haringey this is a huge privilege. My parents are Turkish Cypriot immigrants, who have lived in Wood Green since the late 1960s. I attended local schools - Earlham Primary and Woodside High.It has been a pleasure working with and on behalf of residents. We have achieved a lot, including the Team Noel Park project which focused on fly tipping and Anti Social Behaviour. Many challenges remain, including ensuring that the resident voice is foremost in the regeneration plans for Wood Green. Any changes must be of benefit to those who are already here.

I am the daughter of Cypriot immigrants and grew up on Noel Park Estate.

I now want to fight for all the residents of this ward. My family council home on Farrant Avenue was the anchor and security that allowed me to thrive. I started out in the voluntary sector, moved into local government as a policy officer, and now represent public-sector workers as a Unison branch secretary. I have served as the Labour councillor for Harringay Ward since 2014 and am Chair of the Housing and Regeneration Scrutiny Panel. Education and library services were the key to my development. I was able to achieve this because of the hours I spent in Wood Green Library from the age of 11 in what was then an award-winning Teen Library.

Noel Park has been my home for over 30 years; its vibrant energy, greenery, and diverse cultures make it one of a kind. I know the ward deeply, and I want to stand up and advocate for all who rely on our services. Through my role at Healthwatch Haringey, I look at how services function and how residents’ experiences shape them. My approach to the role of Councillor would be to keep residents at the centre, challenging where things fall short, and working to drive improvements that reflect people’s needs. Fairness, community, and respect are the values that drive me.

As a Tottenham resident for over 40 years, I have lived across our community and raised my children here. They grew into wonderful young adults thanks to our dedicated schools, caring neighbours and the many public services that support families in Tottenham. This community has shaped my life, and I care deeply about its future. Over the years, I have worked hard to support residents, often through challenging circumstances, and I believe I have shown the commitment and ability to help strengthen our community.

If reelected as a local councillor, my priorities are clear: securing more safe and genuinely affordable homes; planting more trees and improving our parks and green spaces; strengthening road safety and reducing dangerous traffic; ensuring cleaner streets; and championing investment in our schools and essential services.

This can only be achieved through genuine partnership with residents, traders, community groups and local businesses. I would be honoured to work with you to shape our ward’s future.

With a background in customer service, I have been a Haringey Councillor since 2002 and have been the borough’s Cabinet Member for Housing and Cabinet Member for the Environment.

I chair the London Housing Consortium, a leading not-for-profit purchasing consortium that provides best value public sector procurement solutions for the construction, refurbishment and maintenance of social housing, schools and other public buildings.

I also represent Haringey as a board member of the Lee Valley Park Authority and am a trustee on the Lee Valley Park Leisure Trust.

My Councillor focus has been listening to residents and improving Northumberland Park.  As Cabinet Member for Communities, I’ve co-designed new plans for the station including better Spurs event co-ordination. I’ve been involved in the Clear, Hold, Build initiative to reduce crime and boost safety. Labour’s values of equality, fairness, and social justice are my values. Working with local residents we’ve achieved closure orders on problem estates, delivered Willoughby Lane’s zebra crossing and added ‘unsuitable for HGVs’ signage. We’ve enhanced our green areas, Somerford Grove and Hartington Park. I’m determined to keep driving progress.

The Council has neglected Northumberland Park. They must open spaces like the Eric Allin Community Centre for use of residents and groups. We need spaces to run community led activities, to support each other, combat isolation and build our community.

We are an alliance of socialists from local grassroots community groups, campaigning groups in the Borough and local community activists. We are committed to transforming local politics in Haringey. We aspire to build an inclusive and democratic alliance to provide a political alternative to the establishment in Haringey and to challenge the rotten Labour Party leadership of the Council that is imposing Austerity on residents. We want to join with and amplify the voices of social justice campaigns across Haringey. We oppose all cuts in services, the wealthy should pay for decent Public Services including housing, health, education. We oppose the privatisation of public services for profit!

On Housing we will:
Expand social home building and provide appropriate accommodation  for all our communities
Call off evictions and the bailiffs and enforce legislation against unlawful and unfair evictions
Campaign for rent controls
Make private landlords implement repairs and refurbishment to provide clean quality rented accommodation for all our residents
Make  a permanent plan to repair and improve all social housing
Change Haringey’s planning priorities to serve Haringey residents not  property developers and speculators
Stop selling off Haringey Council property and assets

On Poverty we will:
Change the narrative! Those in poverty will not be blamed, we will blame those who are making themselves rich off the backs off the poor and the politicians who support the rich
Fight for justice for all benefits claimants and oppose all benefit cuts
Build local community hubs to build solidarity, agency and advocacy for our residents
Campaign for free public transport in London

On the Climate Catastrophe we will
Take all the necessa

Haringey Council is planning £56m worth of cuts which will further decimate services in the Borough. I pledge never to vote for cuts and to demand that the Government funds a needs-based budget for our councils.

We are an alliance of socialists from local grassroots community groups, campaigning groups in the Borough and local community activists. We are committed to transforming local politics in Haringey. We aspire to build an inclusive and democratic alliance to provide a political alternative to the establishment in Haringey and to challenge the rotten Labour Party leadership of the Council that is imposing Austerity on residents. As councillor in Seven Sisters for four years, I’d cherish the opportunity to continue representing residents’ voices to the best of my ability. Our community should have access to high quality services and I’ll ensure our ward receives the necessary funding to implement improvements. My priorities include street cleanliness and condition, housing, personal safety, GP surgeries, the environment and green spaces. We should not accept an east/west divide; from the most privileged to the least, I believe in equality for all. I want to work alongside you to amplify your voice and achieve our shared goals.

I began as Councillor for Seven Sisters Ward in 2022 and it’s been a privilege to serve the ward I call home. I’m a professional social worker with 30 years’ service in mental health services across Adults and Children Services. I’ve witnessed first-hand the deprivation and social inequalities endured by marginalised communities. I’m dedicated to my work and hold regular surgeries, acting swiftly on casework issues. With Councillor Barbara Blake we successfully obtained £150K for a new playground in Paignton Park. I attend local events and I’m committed to advocate for our community, environment and standard of living.

My name is Miguel Baruc Koseleff. Born in Peru, I’ve lived in England for 25 years.
I’m a Linguist and hold a Bachelor’s in multi-lingual Translation and Interpreting. I came to the UK to cover a shortage of Modern Language teachers. I later worked as an Immigration Caseworker for several Solicitor firms. 
I am now retired, and in my ample spare time, I teach Hebrew Bible.

I am standing as a candidate for Reform UK for the different policies the party is promoting in the local elections. Nonetheless, let me tell you about five false MYTHS the extremists are saying about Reform UK.

MYTH 1: “Reform UK is racist and far-right!”

Not true! Reform UK is a democratic party that welcomes members and candidates from all backgrounds and communities. Seven Sisters ward is one of the most diverse areas in Haringey.
In fact, the two candidates the party is presenting for this election are from a Latino and a Jewish-Latino background, respectively! We believe in treating everyone equally, which means focusing on merit, fairness, and the issues that unite our community: safer streets, better and accountable local government. That’s why Raúl and Miguel Baruc, after living both in the UK for over 20 years, are now standing as candidates!

MYTH 2: “Reform UK will privatise the NHS!”

False! Reform UK does not propose privatising the NHS. The party supports a tax-funded, free-at-the-point-of-use health service. Reform UK’s proposals focus on cutting NHS bureaucracy, increasing frontline staffing, and reducing waiting times. At Council level, Reform UK will also push for better-funded social care to relieve pressure on local hospitals and GP surgeries.

MYTH 3: “Reform UK is a single-issue party that only cares about immigration!”

In reality, Reform UK has comprehensive local government policies covering council-tax reduction, planning reform, housing delivery, public safety, and cutting bureaucratic waste. At the local level, Reform UK councillors will focus on the bread-and-butte

My name is Raul Mancera. Born in Colombia, I’ve lived in England for 37 years.
I hold a Master’s degree in Financial Markets & Information Systems (London Metropolitan University) and worked 16 years in Financial company (City of London). I led Pueblito Paisa, a Latin American market association in Seven Sisters, and chaired the FSB London North East Branch.
Currently, a Real Estate Agent.

I am standing as a candidate for Reform UK for the different policies the party is promoting in the local elections. Nonetheless, let me tell you about five false MYTHS the extremists are saying about Reform UK.

MYTH 1: “Reform UK is racist and far-right!”

Not true! Reform UK is a democratic party that welcomes members and candidates from all backgrounds and communities. Seven Sisters ward is one of the most diverse areas in Haringey.
