Listening to residents is key, so I hold regular monthly surgeries. We’re able to resolve issues by fighting for investment in South Tottenham. We’ve secured £250,000 to resolve the longstanding flooding on Markfield Road and £25,000 for a community garden. I’m committed to bringing our communities together to celebrate events like Eid and Purim. Safety is essential for this harmony. That’s why we helped transform a crime hotspot into a space where local children grow vegetables. The council also installed bollards in two busy roads, protecting pedestrians. I’m proud to champion South Tottenham’s diverse communities.

Living in Tottenham since 1997, I’ve spent three terms as a Councillor. I’m dedicated to local residents and committed to equality and social justice. I’ve relentlessly advocated for better services for our residents. Our Haringey Labour Council has built over 1,300 council homes and is modernising existing houses. We’re investing in local parks and improving children’s play areas. As a committed trade union member, I’ll continue to fight for better job protections, and as a Co-operative Party member, I’ll always advocate for food justice as a basic human right. I’m determined to continue improving residents’ quality of life.

As a councillor I’ve worked tirelessly to represent residents, playing my part to improve our borough. I’ve supported residents to set up their first residents association, campaigned successfully for local park funding, organised ward wide weeks of action, increased street cleaning, co-ordinated police meetings to crack down on local ASB; and so much more. In the last few years I’ve taken on a varied number of roles, including charity trustee, chair of the maternity and neonatal voice partnership at the Whittington, and being elected chair of our Children and Young People scrutiny panel.

South Tottenham is my home and my community — it's the only place I'd want to represent.

As a Green councillor, I would focus on:
- Lowering living costs
- Improving local transport and caring for our public spaces
- Making Haringey a more welcoming, safe and beautiful place to live.

If elected, I would bring a strong policy and political background: I currently work for an NGO in sustainable and equitable food policy, working with national government and local authorities on tackling food insecurity and developing local food economies.

I'm an active trade unionist in IWGB, have been involved with Unite and London Renters' Union.

Charlotte Pink has lived in South Tottenham for more than 30 years. As a council tenant and as an LGBTQ+ identifying single parent, she has seen at first hand the devastating effect that indifferent or careless local government can have on the lives of residents. As a councillor, and using her experience as an Ecologist, and environmental educator, she will fight to safeguard the future of Haringey’s green spaces, provide the community with safe and affordable housing, and protect and improve local services. She will take action against rogue landlords and protect renters, stand up for local people against profit-motivated developers, and help protect our streets from fly-tipping and pollution.

I’m standing as a candidate in the South Tottenham Ward for Reform UK because our community deserves far better than we’ve been receiving and I want to be the one to listen and fight for you. I’ve lived in Haringey for forty six years and I’ve seen what’s been happening to this neighbourhood and it saddens me. For too long, hard-working families have been let down by rising Council Tax, ignored concerns, rising crime and struggling businesses. I would be honoured if you would vote for me on the 7th May and allow me to represent you as your Reform UK councillor and let’s make a difference.

Like many young people, I’ve watched national governments struggle with huge challenges. At times, I’ve felt powerless, as though decline is inevitable - but it does not have to be. I’m deeply involved in my trade union as a representative and officer. Locally, I’ve gained an indefatigable drive to help us realise our shared ambitions: decent, affordable housing; strong local communities; high-quality public services; and a recognised workforce with the power to make demands of management. I hope to build on the skills and experience I’ve gained as an activist and organiser to improve the lives of everyone.

I'm a local resident with children at school in St Ann's. I'm standing because I believe in the importance of committed, practical and effective local representation rooted in Labour values

I want to ensure that residents' concerns are heard and addressed. I already have a strong track record of working with the Council to protect and improve St Ann's.

I believe that the only way of achieving change is to have a voice at the table when decisions are being made. I will be that strong voice for you in St Ann's.

Marisha Ray is the Liberal Democrat candidate for the Barnet and Camden London Assembly constituency.

She has spent most of her life in and around the area, growing up and living here. Her family came to the area from India in the 1920’s and has been here ever since as part of a diverse community. She has worked in the UK and France as an IT professional, in procurement and the retail supply chain, as a leading councillor and on the boards and governing bodies of a variety of organisations.

She has a long record of campaigning for a better future for us all in London and asks you to join us in stopping Brexit, campaigning for London to play its part in tackling the climate crisis and for holding the Mayor to account on delivering comfortable, affordable public transport, good quality housing designed for London’s needs and a clean, safe environment.

Bethany Anderson has lived in Stroud Green for 14 years and has raised her two teenage children in the ward. She is an experienced local campaigner and organiser – she was Co-Chair of Friends of Finsbury Park (FOFP) for three years, leading the campaign to expand the skate park, and co-created the Finsbury Park Rewilding Group.

As councillor, she will act on her beliefs: that an equitable society must be built from the local level upwards, with councils taking bold, value-for-money and practical action to deliver genuinely affordable housing, strengthen provision for children with special educational needs, protect parks and nature, ensure kinder and more dignified care for our most vulnerable residents, and create safer, cleaner communities where people feel heard, supported and respected.

As the Chief Executive at a local homelessness charity in Haringey, I help provide resettlement services for vulnerable people across London. As a Haringey councillor over the last four years, I’ve gained skills and knowledge that I will continue to put to good use. It’s a privilege to serve our community and support some of the most marginalised across our borough. I’m passionate about our community and I’m more ready than ever to continue making positive change.

I’m standing to be a strong voice for residents, as I want to help our local community as best I can by ensuring their concerns shape local decisions. I live next door in Crouch End with my husband and two children, who attend local schools. I’ve heard residents’ concerns about housing, crime, anti-social behaviour, and green spaces and parks. I will strive to tackle these issues, and I will work hard to address inequality and improve living standards for Stroud Green. Residents deserve to feel safe and proud of our diverse and wonderful community.

I care deeply about our Stroud Green community and I want to give back by providing a fairer, more equal Haringey for all. Building on the progress already made, I want to focus on local issues in a hands-on manner: safe neighbourhoods, strong local services, protecting our environment, and supporting the businesses that keep Stroud Green thriving. Improving public services and strengthening planning for stronger outcomes has been the focal point of my career, shaping the councillor I want to be: transparent, a listener and a pragmatic problem solver.

Let’s turn Jeremy Corbyn’s words against austerity into action in Haringey!

TUSC candidates are trade unionists, anti-cuts campaigners and Socialist Party members. 

We support Jeremy Corbyn and his anti-austerity manifesto, and we will work alongside any Haringey Labour councillor who wants to put those ideas into practice in Haringey Town Hall. 

There has been a change inside Haringey Labour Party. Now there is a chance to fight for a no-cuts council that ends the cuts and privatisation that has blighted Haringey for years. But Seven Sisters Labour candidates are right-wing, pro-austerity candidates who will carry on with Tory cuts and privatisation.

STOP THE HDV! STOP THE CUTS!

We will fight to:
Stop all cuts to council services and jobs
Reverse cuts to youth services & reopen youth centres
Refuse to increase council tax, rent or service charges to make up for government cuts
Stop privatisation & bring services back in-house
Save Osborne Grove, Haringey’s last remaining publicly-owned nursing home
Stop redevelopment of Wards Corner without social housing and safeguarding the ‘Latin American Corner’ local businesses
Stop the privatisation of Ermine Community Hub in Seven Sisters for adults with learning disabilities and autism
Build a mass campaign involving trade unions, anti-HDV and anti-austerity campaigns to claw back the £155m taken from Haringey by central government.

WE NEED SOCIALIST POLICIES FOR THE BENEFIT OF ALL – NOT THE BILLIONAIRES!

NICK AUVACHE is an active trade unionist and a member of the Socialist Party. He has been active in the Labour Movement for over 30 years and has stood for TUSC previously. He has campaigned against the HDV and cuts in Haringey. As a Unite Rep he has also campaigned against cuts in other boroughs in particular where this has affected housing workers. Having lived in Spain for many years, where he also worked for and alongside the trade union movement, he is keen to build solidarity amongst work

We are the only party to lower taxes, refund the universal credit benefits gap and recover taxes from multinationals to fund our policies. We are the only party to write policies which tackle the root causes of poverty.

We would raise £40.5bn on a 5% turnover tax on multinationals Amazon, Microsoft, Facebook, Starbucks, Shell etc, who send all their profits abroad to avoid paying Corporation Tax (it would be offset against Corporation Tax - so British companies would pay less). 

We would raise £100bn pa by cutting QUANGOS (state funded bodies not run by the Government), which now cost £265bn per year, up from £90bn in 2010.

Save £37.9bn. We would stop public spending on ‘woke’ diversity schemes and partisan campaigns. Also remove some additional ‘social requirements’ for Government contracts which are costing £379bn2 per year, prioritising value for money. Slashing these by 10% would save about £37.9bn. 

About me: I'm married with 3 children. I run a food bank once a week which helps me understand the needs of vulnerable people. I've been involved with various out-reaches over the years including helping the homeless, supporting destitute women and refugees. I’ve campaigned on parental views being represented in school RSE. I’m a member of the SACRE committee in my area. I work part time as a designer. I believe that observing God's ways brings prosperity for all.

I am a doctor and GP trainee, born and raised here, shaped by my heritage and strengthened by my faith. Tottenham is where I learned to fight for my community, for dignity, and for justice, and I have never stopped. I work at the intersection of women’s health, public health and racial inequality. I understand how poor housing, unsafe streets and limited access to services shape people’s health long before they reach a clinic. I have supported international medical aid, education and emergency relief from the UK to Gambia and Gaza.

I’ve lived in Tottenham since 2001 and represented our home since 2018. I wanted to help make this amazing, diverse, exciting place even better. I’m proud to be part of a Labour team that is building high-quality social homes, investing in parks and building a fairer, greener Haringey. We are committed to listening to residents and working alongside them. I’m regularly out on the doorstep locally, talking to residents, listening to what you say we’re doing well and where we can improve. Your feedback helps us make services better for you and focus our resources where they work best.

I’ve lived in Tottenham since 2020 - for three years on West Green Road and for two years off Lawrence Road. As a private renter I moved three times in five years. I’m standing for Haringey Labour as I support tougher protections for private renters through the Landlord Licensing Scheme, and building 3,000 new council houses in the borough by 2031. I pledge to listen to and act on issues all year round, not simply at election time, and be a champion for Tottenham Central. Aside from this election, I work in community engagement for clean energy projects and am a member of GMB trade union.

I’m standing as a Green Party councillor for Tottenham Hale, where I’ve lived for six years, because I’m angry about how inequality and cuts to local services are harming our community, and I want to change that.

As a GP, I see every day how poor housing, insecure work and underfunded services damage health and shorten lives. Labour’s acceptance of austerity has only deepened these problems. It’s not enough to treat the consequences, I want to change the decisions that cause them.

As Clinical Lead for Neighbourhoods, I bring together health, social care and community organisations to work as one team around residents. I focus on practical solutions, building partnerships and tackling inequality at its roots.
I’ve also been involved in grassroots campaigning on social justice and public services, and I’m confident speaking up for communities and challenging decisions that don’t serve them.

What drives me is helping people directly, sorting out casework, fighting for better services and standing up to unfair policies. As a GP, that’s already my day job. As a councillor, I’d bring the same focus: amplifying residents’ voices, winning fairer resources and working relentlessly to reduce inequality in Tottenham Hale.

I am a north London local, having grown up in Hackney, and have lived in Tottenham for the last 11 years. I am a proud resident of Tottenham Hale, and feel fortunate to live among such a warm, friendly and supportive community with a thriving creative sector.
My professional background is in the charity sector, working to create policy change across a range of refugee, health, peace and social justice issues. I have campaigned on issues such as improved maternity care, HIV rights, refugee homelessness and state accountability for deaths in prison and police custody. I volunteer locally with children and young people with SEND and feel strongly about protecting these esrvices, both statutory and in the voluntary sector. 
I was active in the campaign to stop the Haringey Development Vehicle and I'm determined to protect Tottenham Hale's community spaces and ensure Haringey's public assets work for the people of Haringey and not for private developers. I understand the hardships people experience in Tottenham and the sense of being left behind and if elected I will prioritise the urgent need to improve Haringey Council's failing public services so that everyone can lead happy, safe and healthy lives.

I’ve lived in Tottenham for six years, and I’ve always worked as a campaigner in charities. I believe in amplifying people’s voices to tackle inequality and the issues we all care about. Safer roads, clean streets, affordable homes, and the right improvements in Down Lane Park at the heart of the community. Having a Labour Government growing up gave me opportunities. I’ll be a champion for children and young people in Tottenham Hale, and the many local groups doing incredible work supporting them.

I’ve had the privilege of overseeing Down Lane Park’s transformative £7m council investment. Co-designing improvements with residents and establishing local priorities was essential, and I’m proud of what we’ve achieved together. Haringey Labour has built more than 700 sustainable and beautiful council homes at Council rent in Tottenham Hale, and we’re determined to build more. Tackling fly-tipping, reducing pollution and increasing street safety are among your local priorities that we’ll focus on. As a team, we’re committed to keep making improvements and to serve the residents of Tottenham Hale.

I support residents facing unfairness every day. Through my work as a human rights barrister, a trustee for the Bernie Grant Trust and at Citizens Advice Haringey, I know we achieve more when residents, volunteers and councillors work together. My involvement locally has shown me the strength of our community; I believe that fairness and opportunity is what Tottenham Hale needs. I’ll work hard for our residents’ dignity, and to champion our community. Our ward’s uniqueness matters to me. Raised and educated in Tottenham, I know our area’s challenges and potential; I’m committed to meaningful change.

I have been a resident of Haringey for over 35 years, initially within the same Ward I have been nominated to run as a candidate ,Tottenham Hale. I m a mom of three adults and known for my artistic creative work in costume.design, and work as a church and community manager in Tottenham Hale.
From the time I was old enough to vote, I have always felt a strong urge to serve my community in the best way I knew how. By participating in and organising local community events and projects ,by sign posting, giving advice fellow parents , by sitting as parent school governor at Seven Sisters Primary for 14years  and Hornsey Girls for 4 years, by being a local advocate and  assisting in practical ways where I could. I have always felt it my civil duty to vote at elections. I have long felt a strong affinity with the Conservative Party, from the time they made it possible for me to own my own business and home as a young person at 21yrs old through the 0% mortgage scheme. They helped me again through the bounce back loan during COVID. 
They have always promoted financial self empowerment, through self employment  .They recognised the benefits of encouraging young people to become budding entrepreneurs , which in in itself promotes financial stability at a younger age  inspiring many of them to give back to their communities in various ways . I pledge to continue working for our community as I have always been, and be an advocate for our young people. Electing me your local counsellor may assist me to access resources to serve our community even more effectively.

•	Resident in ward since 1983, knows and loves West Green.
•	Passionate about social justice and Palestine.
*       With other Green candidates, wants better rights for private renters; a larger share of housing developments to be for social rent; better repairs and insulation to end cold, damp homes. Evictions and high rents lead to homelessness. Not only one of the worst miseries; it means a huge council bill which could be spent on improving other community services.

*       As a retired academic research worker, hoping to make a strong contribution to the borough team on social care and older people’s issues, after several years of work and publication in this area
 •	Has been in the leadership of Haringey over 50s Forum since 2017, and represents it on the Haringey Joint Partnership Board for Adult Social Services and the Haringey Age Well Board

Living in West Green, I’ve raised my family here and served as a governor at Park View School. As a musician and trade unionist, I’m passionate about putting the arts at the centre of the curriculum, ensuring artists receive the support they need. As Deputy Leader of Haringey Labour, I’m especially proud of our ambitious Council House building programme, which has built over 1000 genuinely affordable homes for local people. West Green is blessed with beautiful parks and I’m excited about the plans for new playgrounds and tree planting. Together, we can make West Green an even better place to live.

Haringey has been my home for more than 30 years. I’ve seen first-hand the strong sense of community that brings people together here. Through my work supporting autistic children and young people, I see every day how important patience, understanding and the right support can be in helping people thrive. Those experiences shape the way I approach community engagement, listening to residents, supporting local services and helping keep West Green a welcoming and thriving place for everyone who lives here. Fairness, community and respect are the values that guide me.

Communities are carrying the burden of a system that is failing them. Years of deep public service cuts and now the cost-of-living crisis have made life even harder, while local rents continue to increase unchecked. Our community deserves better! Elect us to stand against inequality and racism and for decent services.

We are an alliance of socialists from local grassroots community groups, campaigning groups in the Borough and local community activists. We are committed to transforming local politics in Haringey. We aspire to build an inclusive and democratic alliance to provide a political alternative to the establishment in Haringey and to challenge the rotten Labour Party leadership of the Council that is imposing Austerity on residents. We want to join with and amplify the voices of social justice campaigns across Haringey. We oppose all cuts in services, the wealthy should pay for decent Public Services including housing, health, education. We oppose the privatisation of public services for profit!

On Housing we will:
Expand social home building and provide appropriate accommodation for all our communities
Call off evictions and the bailiffs and enforce legislation against unlawful and unfair evictions
Campaign for rent controls
Make private landlords implement repairs and refurbishment to provide clean quality rented accommodation for all our residents
Make a permanent plan to repair and improve all social housing
Change Haringey’s planning priorities to serve Haringey residents not property developers and speculators
Stop selling off Haringey Council property and assets

On Poverty we will:
Change the narrative! Those in poverty will not be blamed, we will blame those who are making themselves rich off the backs off the poor and the politicians who support the rich
Fight for justice for all benefits claimants and oppose all benefit cuts
Build local community hubs to build solidarity, agency and advocacy for our residents
Campaign for free public transport i

Haringey Council has had a £53m cut in core funding since the financial crisis. If the government can bail out the nanks they can bail out Haringey Council. If elected I will never vote for cuts.

implement policies that stop children going hungry and work with community organisations to ensure all have good nutrition and dignity in accessing food
Support all community food growing projects and initiatives
Refurbish and expand community spaces and centres ensuring all local groups have the community spaces they want
Develop play, sport and recreation spaces for all our children and adults

On unity against racism & discrimination we will:
Strengthen language support and boost integration of communities
Decolonize local education, ban exclusions and expand all education for migrants to prosper in Haringey because migrants are welcome in Haringey
Not co-operate with Prevent or Immigration raids or police targeting minorities
Defend Trans rights, women's rights and disability rights

On Israel's Genocide we will:
End all investment in businesses that are trading with or in Israel
Develop and implement and policy of ethical investment that stops investment in businesses associated with military, war and genocide
Stop banking with Barclays
Support Workers' direct action boycott of Israel, war, genocide and campaign for all trade-unions in Haringey to support the boycott of genocide across the world
Support the self-determination of the Palestinian people and the Kurdish people

We stand firmly on the principle of building a just and inclusive society—one that is anti-racist, anti-fascist, anti-austerity, anti-war and anti-imperialist. We are committed to defending and expanding high-quality, publicly owned services which are developed in meaningful partnership with the residents who need them

Mike Hodges has lived in Tottenham since 1992 and spent over 25 years teaching primary school children. He has continued to serve his community since. As a committee member of Tottenham’s community owned pub The Antwerp Arms he obtained grants to install solar panels, and has worked to fund other initiatives.

He is a housing campaigner, having fought for repairs and fire safety in local council homes, and is an active member of Unite Community, campaigning against government attacks on disabled people.

As a White Hart Lane councillor, his focus will be on resolving residents’ problems, and he will advocate for children with special educational needs and their parents.

Marie Kristensen moved to the UK fourteen years ago, and has lived in Haringey for all of that time. She has worked in Special Needs Education for over a decade and is currently a SEND team leader for another local authority. Marie is standing as a councillor so she can bring that experience in service to Haringey’s young people directly, as well as focusing on housing, poverty, and just development.

As a resident of almost 40 years with an allotment on De Quincey Road, I’m frequently out and about White Hart Lane. I raised a family here, and I worked supporting parents of excluded school children. I’m Assistant Branch Secretary for Schools in Barnet Unison and have 15 years’ experience in managing casework in Family Services, problem solving, sign posting and advocating. I’ve strong negotiating skills that I’d use to be a strong voice for White Hart Lane. I’d love the chance to use my experience and passion for being a voice for those who are less heard, to serve our community.

Shaan Rafiq is proud to come from a tradition of community care – his mother was a dinner lady who became a foster carer, his father a cab driver who became a teacher. Today, Shaan is a software engineer and a foster carer himself. Living in Haringey for the past 3 years, Shaan is standing because he wants local people to have a councillor who understands how to help residents navigate systems that can often feel confusing or unwelcoming. He is particularly interested in supporting those who need help to speak up for themselves, and in making sure council decisions are guided by fairness rather than profit.

Tough times demand experienced leadership and that’s why I’m standing again, to use every bit of my local knowledge to deliver more funding for our green spaces, safer roads and streets for our families. My pragmatic leadership delivers for our community. I was born here. I grew up here and since 2022, I’ve served as White Hart Lane Councillor, as a recent Mayor and former Chair of the Pensions Board. The economic challenges ahead are real but we’ve achieved change together. Let’s finish what we started, we can build a White Hart Lane that keeps excelling for our community.

White Hart Lane’s diverse and welcoming communities deserve safety and economic growth. I co-founded a local Community Centre in Tottenham. I’ve seen how strong Labour-led Haringey Council services improve lives. I want to build on that, with even more investment in social housing, green spaces, and fair economic chances for all. Haringey’s diversity is its greatest strength - I want to nurture it. I’m running to ensure the council reflects every community it serves. I’m dedicated to building trust and working with residents. Together, we can keep White Hart Lane a place where all feel heard and represented.

I’m standing for Reform UK because our community deserves better. I’ve lived and worked in Haringey most of my life, as a care worker, editor and librarian. ‘The Lane’ and I go back a long way – as a kid I would walk the length of it with my mates to watch Spurs. I’ve seen what’s happened to this neighbourhood, and it saddens me.

For too long, hardworking families have been let down. Council tax keeps rising, local people’s concerns are ignored, crime and anti-social behaviour increase, and local businesses struggle. I know what matters to the people of this ward, and it would be the honour of my life to be your White Hart Lane councillor.

It’s been a privilege to be your councillor.

I’ve stood up for our green spaces, worked to tackle anti-social behaviour, pushed for improvements to landlord‑owned properties, and fought for better parking, housing, care, and road safety.

As a cabinet member, I’ve led Haringey’s award-winning gambling harms work, influencing the government and becoming the first borough to join the Coalition to End Gambling Advertising. I’ve overseen the opening of our mental health hub, Canning Crescent’s Roger Sylvester Centre, and we now have regular wellbeing walks and new school streets.

Together, let’s make Woodside the best it can be.

I grew up in Haringey and it shaped who I am. Coming from a Kurdish Alevi background, I learned early what it feels like to be unheard. I’m determined that every resident has someone who will stand up for them. Through working at the Alevi Centre, I’ve supported people with housing, family and personal challenges. I’m someone others can rely on. As I’ve lived here most of my life, I understand the issues people deal with every day. If elected, I’ll be a visible councillor who listens and takes action. I want to serve, and give back to our community.

As your Woodside councilor, I’ve delivered on your priorities. Together with Haringey’s Labour Council, we’ve delivered a new children’s playground in Woodside Park, with another £350k being invested in WHL recreation. Along Perth Road and nearby streets, we’ve worked with residents to co-design traffic calming measures and make our roads safer. And on ASB, we’ve teamed up with local people, council officers, and the police to tackle problems on Lordship Lane and make our streets safer for our community. There is much more to do, and I want to be that person representing your voice.
